# Release Automation Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Make `scripts/release.sh <version>` turn a clean `master` into a tagged, notarized, published GitHub Release with auto-generated CHANGELOG and release notes.

**Architecture:** git-cliff converts Conventional Commits into `CHANGELOG.md` and per-version release notes. A new `scripts/release.sh` orchestrates changelog → commit → tag → build (via the unchanged `build-app.sh`) → push → `gh release create`, with a dry-run preview and a confirmation gate before any irreversible step.

**Tech Stack:** Bash, [git-cliff](https://git-cliff.org) (TOML + Tera templates), GitHub CLI (`gh`), existing `scripts/build-app.sh`.

---

## Spec

`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-release-automation-design.md`

## File Structure

- **Create** `cliff.toml` — git-cliff config: which commits map to which sections, body template (scope-bold, hash links, compare links).
- **Create** `CHANGELOG.md` — generated by git-cliff from full tag history (`v0.1.0`…`v0.2.0`), accumulated going forward.
- **Create** `scripts/release.sh` — release orchestrator. Owns changelog/tag/push/release; calls `build-app.sh` for the build.
- **Modify** `README.md` — rewrite the "Notarized release" section to document the one-command `release.sh` flow.

`scripts/build-app.sh` is intentionally **unchanged** — it keeps its single "build/notarize/zip" responsibility.

## Prerequisites (one-time, manual)

These are environment setup, not code. The first task verifies they exist and the executing engineer installs anything missing:

- `brew install git-cliff`
- `gh auth login --hostname github.com` (personal account `p4569zz@gmail.com`) — `gh` is currently only authed to the company GHE host `github.jobis.co`.

---

### Task 1: git-cliff config (`cliff.toml`)

**Files:**
- Create: `cliff.toml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Ensure git-cliff is installed**

Run: `command -v git-cliff || brew install git-cliff`
Expected: a path is printed (e.g. `/opt/homebrew/bin/git-cliff`). If not, brew installs it.

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `cliff.toml`**

Create `cliff.toml` with exactly this content:

```toml
# git-cliff configuration — https://git-cliff.org
# Only feat/fix surface in the changelog; scope is rendered as a bold prefix.

[changelog]
header = """
# Changelog

All notable changes to Parallel are documented here.
"""
body = """
{% set repo = "https://github.com/BottlePumpkin/Parallel" -%}
{% if version -%}
## {% if previous.version %}[{{ version }}]({{ repo }}/compare/{{ previous.version }}...{{ version }}){% else %}{{ version }}{% endif %} ({{ timestamp | date(format="%Y-%m-%d") }})
{% else -%}
## [Unreleased]
{% endif -%}
{% for group, commits in commits | group_by(attribute="group") %}
### {{ group }}
{% for commit in commits %}
- {% if commit.scope %}**{{ commit.scope }}:** {% endif %}{{ commit.message | upper_first }} ([`{{ commit.id | truncate(length=7, end="") }}`]({{ repo }}/commit/{{ commit.id }}))
{%- endfor %}
{% endfor %}
"""
trim = true
footer = ""

[git]
conventional_commits = true
filter_unconventional = true
filter_commits = true
split_commits = false
sort_commits = "newest"
tag_pattern = "v[0-9]*"
commit_parsers = [
    { message = "^feat", group = "Features" },
    { message = "^fix", group = "Bug Fixes" },
    { message = ".*", skip = true },
]
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Validate against existing tag history**

Run: `git-cliff --tag v0.2.0 | head -60`
Expected: Markdown with `## [0.2.0]...` style headings, a `### Features` and/or `### Bug Fixes` section, entries like `- **views:** ...` with a `` [`abc1234`] `` hash link. NO `### Documentation` / `### Chores` sections (docs/chore/test/refactor are skipped).

If the template whitespace looks off (blank lines doubled, headings glued together), adjust the `body` whitespace-control markers (`-%}` / `{%-`) and re-run until it reads cleanly.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add cliff.toml
git commit -m "build: git-cliff config for changelog generation"
```

---

### Task 2: Backfill `CHANGELOG.md`

**Files:**
- Create: `CHANGELOG.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Generate the full changelog from history**

Run: `git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md`
Expected: `CHANGELOG.md` is created covering every tag from `v0.1.0` through `v0.2.0`, newest first.

- [ ] **Step 2: Eyeball the result**

Run: `head -40 CHANGELOG.md`
Expected: `# Changelog` header, then `## [0.2.0]...` and earlier versions, each with Features / Bug Fixes only. Confirm no skipped types (docs/chore/test/refactor) leaked in.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "docs: backfill CHANGELOG from v0.1.0..v0.2.0"
```

---

### Task 3: Release orchestrator (`scripts/release.sh`)

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/release.sh`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `scripts/release.sh`**

Create `scripts/release.sh` with exactly this content:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Release orchestrator: changelog -> commit -> tag -> build -> push -> GitHub Release.
# Usage:  scripts/release.sh [--dry-run] <X.Y.Z>
# Build/notarize/zip is delegated to scripts/build-app.sh (unchanged).
set -euo pipefail

REPO="BottlePumpkin/Parallel"
GH_HOST="github.com"
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

die() { echo "❌ $*" >&2; exit 1; }

DRY_RUN=false
if [[ "${1:-}" == "--dry-run" ]]; then DRY_RUN=true; shift; fi

VERSION="${1:-}"
[[ -n "$VERSION" ]] || die "Usage: release.sh [--dry-run] <X.Y.Z>   e.g. release.sh 0.3.0"
[[ "$VERSION" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]] || die "Version must be X.Y.Z (got '$VERSION')"
TAG="v$VERSION"

# --- Preflight (fail fast) ---
command -v git-cliff >/dev/null || die "git-cliff not installed — brew install git-cliff"
command -v gh >/dev/null || die "gh not installed — brew install gh"
gh auth status --hostname "$GH_HOST" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
  || die "gh not authenticated to $GH_HOST — run: gh auth login --hostname $GH_HOST"
[[ "$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)" == "master" ]] || die "Not on master"
git diff --quiet && git diff --cached --quiet || die "Working tree not clean — commit or stash first"
! git rev-parse "$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "Tag $TAG already exists"

if [[ -z "${NOTARY_PROFILE:-}" || -z "${SIGN_IDENTITY:-}" ]]; then
  echo "⚠️  NOTARY_PROFILE/SIGN_IDENTITY unset — this release will be UNNOTARIZED."
fi

# --- Preview the release notes for this version ---
echo "==> git-cliff suggested next version: $(git-cliff --bumped-version 2>/dev/null || echo 'n/a')"
NOTES="$(git-cliff --unreleased --tag "$TAG" --strip header)"
echo "================ Release notes for $TAG ================"
echo "$NOTES"
echo "======================================================="

if $DRY_RUN; then
  echo "✅ dry-run complete — nothing committed, pushed, or published."
  exit 0
fi

read -r -p "Proceed to build, push, and publish $TAG? [y/N] " ans
[[ "$ans" == "y" || "$ans" == "Y" ]] || die "Aborted by user."

# --- Changelog + commit + tag (local, still reversible until push) ---
git-cliff --tag "$TAG" -o CHANGELOG.md
git add CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "docs: changelog for $TAG"
git tag "$TAG"

# --- Build / notarize / zip ---
./scripts/build-app.sh "$VERSION"
ZIP="build/Parallel-$VERSION-mac.zip"
[[ -f "$ZIP" ]] || die "Expected $ZIP not found after build — aborting before push."

# --- Irreversible: push + publish ---
git push origin master --follow-tags
gh release create "$TAG" "$ZIP" \
  --repo "$REPO" \
  --title "$TAG" \
  --notes "$NOTES"

echo "✅ Released $TAG → https://github.com/$REPO/releases/tag/$TAG"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Make it executable**

Run: `chmod +x scripts/release.sh`
Expected: no output; `ls -l scripts/release.sh` shows the `x` bit.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify the dry-run path end-to-end (no side effects)**

Run: `./scripts/release.sh --dry-run 0.3.0`
Expected: preflight passes (or fails with a clear message if git-cliff/gh/auth missing), prints the `### Features` / `### Bug Fixes` notes for the commits since `v0.2.0`, then `✅ dry-run complete`. Afterward, `git status` is clean and `git tag` does NOT contain `v0.3.0` — confirming nothing was written.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify a bad version is rejected**

Run: `./scripts/release.sh --dry-run 0.3` ; `echo "exit=$?"`
Expected: `❌ Version must be X.Y.Z (got '0.3')` and `exit=1`.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/release.sh
git commit -m "build: release.sh — one-command changelog + notarized GitHub Release"
```

---

### Task 4: Update README "Notarized release" section

**Files:**
- Modify: `README.md` (the "### Notarized release (Apple Developer required)" section)

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace the section body**

In `README.md`, the "### Notarized release (Apple Developer required)" section currently ends with:

```markdown
Then every release is one command:

​```bash
./scripts/build-app.sh 0.2.0
​```

It signs with hardened runtime, submits to Apple, waits for notarization (1-5 min), staples the ticket onto the `.app`, and re-zips. Users double-click to launch on any Mac — no warnings.
```

Replace that trailing `Then every release is one command:` block (from "Then every release is one command:" through the "Users double-click…" paragraph) with:

```markdown
For a build-only `.app` (no publish), `./scripts/build-app.sh 0.2.0` signs with
hardened runtime, submits to Apple, waits for notarization (1-5 min), staples the
ticket onto the `.app`, and re-zips.

To cut and publish a full release in one command:

​```bash
brew install git-cliff               # one-time
gh auth login --hostname github.com  # one-time (public repo lives on github.com)

./scripts/release.sh 0.3.0
​```

`release.sh` regenerates `CHANGELOG.md` from Conventional Commits, previews the
release notes, and — after you confirm — commits the changelog, tags `v0.3.0`,
builds + notarizes via `build-app.sh`, pushes, and creates the GitHub Release with
the notarized zip attached. The generated notes become the release body that the
in-app **Check for Updates** shows. Use `./scripts/release.sh --dry-run 0.3.0` to
preview without pushing.
```

(The triple-backticks above are shown with a zero-width marker so they render in this plan — write them as normal ` ``` ` fences in the README.)

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the section reads correctly**

Run: `sed -n '/### Notarized release/,/## Optional/p' README.md`
Expected: the section now documents `release.sh` with the one-time setup and dry-run note, and no longer claims `build-app.sh` alone is "every release."

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add README.md
git commit -m "docs: document release.sh one-command release flow"
```

---

## Self-Review Notes

- **Spec coverage:** cliff.toml rules (Task 1) ✓; CHANGELOG backfill v0.1.0…v0.2.0 (Task 2) ✓; release.sh preflight/preview/confirm/build/push/release flow (Task 3) ✓; dry-run validation (Task 3 Steps 3-4) ✓; unnotarized warning + tag-exists + dirty-tree + auth guards (Task 3 Step 1) ✓; README update (Task 4) ✓; github.com auth via personal account documented (Prerequisites + Task 4) ✓.
- **Manual version arg:** matches spec (no auto-bump; `--bumped-version` printed for reference only).
- **Naming consistency:** `TAG="v$VERSION"`, `ZIP="build/Parallel-$VERSION-mac.zip"` (matches `build-app.sh` output `build/Parallel-<ver>-mac.zip`), repo `BottlePumpkin/Parallel`, host `github.com` — used consistently across release.sh and README.
- **Known soft spot:** git-cliff Tera template whitespace can need a small tweak; Task 1 Step 3 explicitly validates and adjusts before anything depends on it.
